name: The Lighthouse Cafe and Bookshop
description: This was a project in sophomore year to create a website for a fictional restaurant.
featuredDesc: For this website, I chose the city of Genoa for its iconic lighthouse, and this website has my first working contact form. Additionally, I created the menu myself, as well as several other graphics present. This was my largest project of sophomore year.
reflection: In this project I learned how to make a fully functional contact form, as well as reusable components.

name: Alternate Aircraft Archive
description: A website designed to house my many fictonal military vehicle drawings. I never completed it.

name: Tromso Vacation Website
description: A website built for Tromso in Norway, as a destination site.
featuredDesc: This was a vacation website built about Tromso in Norway. It had my first usage of autogenerated HTML components, as well as heavy image texturing and special effects.
reflection: In this project I learned how to use cookies and a settings menu, as well as heavy image texturization.

name: Perfect Pumpkin Pie
description: A recipe for the perfect Pumkpin Pie.

name: Prohibition Essay
description: An essay for English 2, built as a website.

name: The Solar System
description: A guide to the solar system
featuredDesc: One of my earliest projects, I used GIFs heavily and an image map to make the website more interactive. Already I was experimenting.
reflection: In this project I learned to use Image Maps, and learned about SPAA's. From this I gained the knowledge of image maps and animated GIFs for interactivity.

name: TCA1 WDPP SG
description: A study guide for the TCA 1 of Honors Web Design.

name: Kelly Johnson Tribute
description: A tribute website for Kelly Johnson of Lockheed Martin

name: How to Embed
description: A website designed to help explain how to embed videos and maps.

name: Falling Seasons
description: A website showcasing CSS animations of seasonal artefacts.

name: Chemistry Study Guide
description: A chemistry study guide to the first ten elements.

name: Dracula's Castle
description: A click-through 'game' built early on.

name: Sophomore Portfolio
description: My portfolio of sophomore year, and it also contains several photographs.

name: Marlboro Farmer's Market
description: A redesigned brochure website for the Marlboro Farmers' Market

name: My First Bootstrap
description: My first webpage using Bootstrap, an HTML / CSS framework.

name: Music and Art Academy
description: A redesigned website for the Music and Art Academy. Built as the Work Based Learning Project.
featuredDesc: My most advanced website, this is a redesign of the website of the Music and Art Academy school. It incorporates a Google Sheet as a backend database and emailer, and has several advanced concepts with pulling files from Google Drive and autonomously handling the construction of the website from that data. I am very proud of it.
reflection: In this project I learned to dummy-proof my websites, offering tons of navigation options, as well as several UI elements specifically designed to be easier to use than normal.

name: Weekly News Site
description: A website built to aid the delivery of weekly updates through Marlboro High School.

name: TXT-DRIVER.exe
description: An entirely ASCII-based game, a dodgem-cars style retro game.
featuredDesc: My most advanced game yet, this is a dodgem opposite lane style retro game continuing the trend of ASCII interfaces, only now with a configurable setup, multiple levels, and more complexity in gameplay and usage.
reflection: In this project I learned how to make a monolithic game engine, complete with subclasses and a dynamically generated game area.

name: Team Roster
description: A website showcasing the major Star Wars characters as a roster.

name: Text Pong
description: A retro Pong-style game built with ASCII characters.

name: RIT College Website
description: A senior college-assistance website guide to RIT.

name: Dreidel Website
description: A holiday website which spins the dreidel.

name: NJIT jQuery Project
description: A carousel of tanks, styled in retro with jQuery.

name: Senior Portfolio
description: WEB-CEPTION!

name: NJIT Check Movies
description: A NJIT assignment to build a movie list.

name: Little Bird Toy Company
description: The final assessment for Junior year

name: Wildcard Website
description: A stack calculator displayed in ASCII, as my first ASCII interface.
featuredDesc: A number of firsts for me: my first calculator, and my first ASCII interface, automatically generating the boxes from letters and numbers instead of elements. It also involves a complex system to 'click through' the overlaying div.
reflection: In this project I had built my first iteration of the ASCII interface which I would go on to use later. This project also taught me what a stack calculator is.

name: Hangman
description: A shamrock-themed hangman game.

name: Monthly Website Gallery
description: My Junior year portfolio.

name: Summer-tainment Site
description: My first Junior year website, designed to recap all of my summer activities.

name: Look Ma, I'm Famous
description: A website about Graf Zeppelin.

name: TCA1
description: A website of CSS animations for Junior Year TCA 1.

name: Madlibs
description: A madlibs site with 4 stories.

name: Name Generator
description: A medieval warrior name generator.

name: Magic 8 Ball
description: A magic 8 ball website.

name: Rock Paper Scissors Matrix
description: The Matrix-edition of Rock Paper Scissors. It is very computer-intensive.
featuredDesc: A Matrix edition of Rock Paper Scissors, this website put my computer to the test to automatically generate the jumping boxes--hundreds of them--to create the digitization effect. Totally worth it, though.
reflection: In this project, I used JS to create dynamic styles, animations, and html elements to create specific effects, teaching me the techniques to create visuals normally impossible.

name: President Website
description: A website about our former president, Calvin Coolidge.

name: To Do List
description: A website to-do list.

name: Reversi
description: A working 1 - 2 player game of Othello / Reversi.
featuredDesc: My most advanced game for the time, it had both two player and single player bot mode. It taught me recursive functions, as well as game optimisation and digital web-game design. I have lost to the bot on numerous occasions.
reflection: In this project, I had built my second game-bot ever, and created a system for it to be 'smart'. This also coupled with a monolithic game engine and recursive programming for the first time!

name: Schedules
description: A website to display school schedules.

name: Battleship
description: A single-player battleship game.

name: KDTree Algorithm
description: A work-in-progress numerical vector-search algorithm.
featuredDesc: A highly complex nearest-neighbor vector-search algorithm, this KDTree is the latest step in my learning of how these systems work. I am currently optimizing it for work on quantizing images.
reflection: This project taught me data management and memory management, as well as more in depth recursive algorithms as well as partial recursion and quickselect.

name: Hello World
description: My first website ever.

name: Let's Go Camping
description: A website about going camping!

name: An Epic Guide to Epic Quests
description: A website instructing noble adventurers about how to best go about their adventures.

name: Chess Board
description: A project to demonstrate HTML tables.

name: Halloween Movie
description: The sophomore Halloween website, about movie recommendations.

name: Nav Bar
description: My first nav bar.

name: NJ Lighthouses
description: A website showcasing NJ lighthouses. Uses one of the first JS snippets I ever implemented.

name: Puppies
description: A bootstrap exercise with carousels.

name: Album Covers
description: A CSS exercise with Album Covers.

name: Sloan Kettering Cancer Initiative
description: A brochure style website for MSK Cancer Disparities Initiative.

description: A carousel tribute page to MLK Jr.
